page: OVHcloud r3-256 is a memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by dedicated AMD EPYC Milan processors. The configuration features 32 physical cores running at 2.3 GHz with a single thread per core, delivering dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes 8 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, the r3-256 demonstrates strong performance, placing in the top 25% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, as well as Passmark CPU and memory scores. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with cached read-only small block speeds reaching 4744.5 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 279.53 GB/sec. Database operations and key-value store performance are also highly rated, with Redis SET operations achieving 75.95 million operations per second. However, static web serving throughput and medium-sized LLM inference fall into the average tier, representing potential performance tradeoffs for those specific workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation combined with a dense memory profile offers a favorable benchmark-per-resource ratio for memory-bound applications. The absence of hyperthreading ensures predictable execution times, which is advantageous for database hosting, in-memory caching, and large-scale data processing. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as Redis deployments, relational database operations, software compilation, and multi-threaded data decompression.
description: OVHcloud r3-256 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage with 8 Gbps network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates strong top-25% performance in multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 75.95 million operations per second. Conversely, static web serving and medium-scale LLM inference perform in the average tier. This resource density and dedicated CPU allocation make the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases, and complex data processing tasks.
og_description: OVHcloud r3-256 is a memory-optimized server with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong database and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.

256 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
400 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 8 Gbps network bandwidth
